title: AN ACT SEPARATING THE PEDRO T. MENDIOLA SR. MEMORIAL NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL √¢¬Ä¬ì CAMINAWIT ANNEX IN THE MUNICIPALITY OF SAN JOSE, PROVINCE OF OCCIDENTAL MINDORO FROM THE PEDRO T. MENDIOLA SR. MEMORIAL NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, CONVERTING IT INTO AN INDEPENDENT NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL TO BE KNOWN AS CAMINAWIT NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

chunks.text:
109 OG No. 5, 486 (February 4, 2013)

[ REPUBLIC ACT NO. 10275, November 15, 2012 ]

AN ACT SEPARATING THE PEDRO T. MENDIOLA SR. MEMORIAL NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL √¢¬Ä¬ì CAMINAWIT ANNEX IN THE MUNICIPALITY OF SAN JOSE, PROVINCE OF OCCIDENTAL MINDORO FROM THE PEDRO T. MENDIOLA SR. MEMORIAL NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, CONVERTING IT INTO AN INDEPENDENT NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL TO BE KNOWN AS CAMINAWIT NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representatives of the Philippines in Congress assembled:

SECTION 1.

Separation and Conversion into a National High School.

√¢¬Ä¬ì The Pedro T. Mendiola Sr. Memorial National High School √¢¬Ä¬ì Caminawit Annex in the Municipality of San Jose, Province of Occidental Mindoro is hereby separated from the Pedro T. Mendiola Sr. Memorial National High School and converted into an independent national high school to be known as Caminawit National High School.

SEC. 2.

Transfer of Assets and Liabilities. √¢¬Ä¬ì

All personnel, assets, liabilities and records of the Pedro T. Mendiola Sr. Memorial National High School √¢¬Ä¬ì Caminawit Annex are hereby transferred to and absorbed by the Caminawit National High School.

SEC. 3.

Appropriations.

√¢¬Ä¬ì The Secretary of Education shall immediately include in the Department√¢¬Ä¬ôs program the operationalization of the Caminawit National High School, the initial funding of which shall be charged against the current year√¢¬Ä¬ôs appropriations of the Pedro T. Mendiola Sr. Memorial National High School √¢¬Ä¬ì Caminawit Annex. Thereafter, the amount necessary for the continued operation of the school shall be included in the annual General Appropriations Act.

SEC. 4.

Implementation. √¢¬Ä¬ì

The Secretary of Education shall issue such rules and regulations as may be necessary to carry out the purpose of this Act.

SEC. 5.

Effectivity.

√¢¬Ä¬ì This Act shall take effect fifteen (15) days after its publication in the

Official Gazette.

Approved,

(Sgd.) JUAN PONCE ENRILE

President of the Senate

(Sgd.)

FELICIANO BELMONTE JR.

Speaker of the House

of Representatives

This Act which originated in the House of Representatives was finally passed by the House of Representatives and the Senate on October 3, 2011 and August 28, 2012, respectively.

(Sgd.) EMMA LIRIO-REYES

Secretary of the Senate

(Sgd.) MARILYN B. BARUA-YAP

Secretary General

House of Representatives

Approved:

NOV 15 2012

(Sgd.) BENIGNO S. AQUINO III

President of the Philippines
